name: GPT Researcher
what_it_does: Autonomous research agent that plans research questions, scrapes and validates multiple web and local sources, then synthesizes findings into detailed cited reports exceeding 2000 words with 20+ sources.
use_cases: Market research and competitive analysis with cited sources
Academic literature review and synthesis across multiple papers
Investment due diligence and company analysis
News trend analysis aggregating multiple perspectives
not_for: Simple single-query lookups where a search engine suffices
Offline-only environments with no web access
best_when: You need thorough, multi-source research synthesized into a cohesive report with citations, and can tolerate ~30-second to 5-minute processing times and per-query LLM costs.
avoid_when: You need instant answers, have no budget for LLM API costs, or need fully reproducible deterministic outputs.

alternatives.name: Tavily
alternatives.note: Simpler web search API without autonomous agent orchestration

alternatives.name: Perplexity
alternatives.note: Hosted research assistant with faster responses but less depth

auth.notes: Requires OPENAI_API_KEY and TAVILY_API_KEY environment variables. Supports other LLM providers (Anthropic, etc.) via custom base URL configuration.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited local use; costs come from upstream LLM and search APIs (~$0.40 per deep research query with o3-mini)
pricing.notes: Open source Apache 2.0. Operational costs depend on chosen LLM provider and web search API.

performance.rate_limits: Dependent on upstream API rate limits (OpenAI, Tavily)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Standard Python exceptions; no structured error codes documented
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Research queries are non-deterministic by nature
known_agent_gotchas: Deep research mode takes ~5 minutes and costs ~$0.40 per query
Requires both OpenAI and Tavily API keys minimum
Results vary between runs due to web source availability
